text:
Hans sat at his table, staring out the window into the evening. It was quiet in the small town inn he worked at. There was always the clinking of dishes, but mostly it was silence.

The clock struck nine in the morning, waking him from his restless sleep. He rubbed his eyes, pulled on his coat, and grabbed his breakfast as he bolted from the inn. The chill in the air suggested rain later; he hoped for a morning walk to clear his head.

He walked down the cobblestone streets, the fog thick and hushed. A stray dog joined him, its nose twitching at the scent of bread. He bought a loaf of bread and offered it to the pup.

After a few blocks, his boots splashed through puddles as he made his way to the river. The water was clear and calm, reflection dancing on its surface.

"Nature," he murmured, letting out a long breath.

The dog sat beside him for a while,
